actionable_feedback: Provide a CSV export (or separate CSV files) of the key spreadsheet sheets (triage and sensitivity) alongside the .xlsx so automated runtimes can parse values without openpyxl.

Expose the decision-rule formulas and boolean comparison cells in the workbook (or include a small 'checks' sheet with explicit formulas such as =PRB>0.85 and =SINR<12) so graders/verifiers can verify logic programmatically.
Add explicit unit labels to spreadsheet column headers and, where helpful, adjacent 'units' cells (e.g., 'PRB util (%)', 'Throughput (Mbps)') to remove unit ambiguity.
Document the exact prioritization scoring method in the workbook (e.g., +1 per rule hit or weighted scoring and any tie-breakers) so the mapping from rule hits to P1/P2/etc. is reproducible.
Add at least one automated sensitivity scenario row inside the spreadsheet (e.g., simulated PRB A = 86%) that triggers recalculation and demonstrates reprioritization automatically.

For the highest-priority follow-up actions (Cell B), include expected indicator thresholds and collection windows for the recommended checks (e.g., 'busy-hour PRB >85% for 3 consecutive days', 'SINR 10th percentile <12 dB over busy hour'), and note any safe rollback or mitigations if field changes are applied.

Include a short README or a one-paragraph 'repro notes' in RF_note.md that points to the exact spreadsheet cells/sheets and lists the provided auxiliary files (CSV, screenshots) so verifiers can reproduce results quickly.
artifact_specific_comments: RF_note.md: clear and well-documented; reference the exact spreadsheet cells in-line to improve traceability.
RAN_capacity_triage.xlsx: presence confirmed but programmatic parsing was not available in this environment; include CSV/screenshot exports and explicit formula cells to aid automated verification.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are complete and internally consistent: RF_note.md documents inputs, calculations, decision logic, priorities, and sensitivity notes. Primary limitation is environment inability to programmatically parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable), so reproducibility and automated checks would benefit from small additional artifacts and explicit spreadsheet formula exposure.
